content.text: CSS color and background properties control the visual appearance of every element on the page. Understanding the modern color formats, the full
shorthand, and how to structure color as design tokens will make your stylesheets both correct and maintainable.

attrs.alt: CSS Colors and Backgrounds: color formats, gradient types, background shorthand, color tokens, and background-size/position

content.text: CSS supports multiple color syntaxes. Each has different ergonomics and gamut coverage:
Perceptually uniform; best for design tokens (2023+)
147 named colors; avoid except for transparent/currentColor
Cascade control, not color values per se
OKLCH is the modern choice for design systems.

It is perceptually uniform (equal numeric steps look equally different visually), supports the P3 wide color gamut, and makes programmatic color manipulation (lighter/darker) predictable. HSL is still widely used and perfectly fine for most projects.
The 147 CSS named colors include a few genuinely useful values:
The two most fundamental color properties:
sets the foreground color for text and also the value of
, which SVG fills and borders can reference.
property accepts URLs and CSS gradient functions. Gradients are treated as images:
Load an image file as the background:
Scales image to cover the entire element; may clip
Scales to fit inside the element; may letterbox
Stretches to full width; height scales proportionally
Tile with equal spacing; never clips
Tile and scale to avoid partial tiles
CSS allows multiple background layers, separated by commas. The first layer is on top:
creates a parallax-like effect but triggers paint on every scroll frame. It also does not work inside CSS
containers, and can cause performance issues on mobile. Use sparingly.
CSS Custom Properties for Color Tokens
Modern design systems store colors as CSS custom properties on
, then reference them everywhere. This centralizes the palette and enables dark mode with a single override:
Angular: Theming with CSS Custom Properties
In Angular components, define tokens in the global stylesheet (
) and use them in component styles. The
boundary does not block custom property inheritance:
Two patterns for dark mode in Angular: CSS media query (automatic) and class/attribute toggling (user-controlled):
For user-controlled dark mode, store the preference in
and apply the class/attribute in the Angular app initializer or directly on
to prevent flash on page load.

Color Functions: mix() and relative color syntax
Modern CSS adds powerful color manipulation functions that let you derive colors programmatically:
is supported in all modern browsers since 2023. Relative color syntax (
) arrived in Chrome 119 and Safari 16.4 — check caniuse.com before using in production without a fallback.
controls how far the background extends, and
WCAG 2.1 specifies minimum contrast ratios between text and its background:
Test contrast ratios with browser DevTools (the color picker shows the ratio in real time) or tools like the WCAG Color Contrast Checker. Some rules for building accessible color systems:
Never convey information by color alone
— use icons, text, or patterns alongside color.
Test your palette at both light and dark mode
— a color that passes in light mode may fail in dark mode.
Avoid pure white text on colored buttons
— very high saturation backgrounds paired with pure white can have surprising contrast ratios. Verify with a tool.
— red/green distinctions fail for ~8% of male users. Use tools like Stark or Chrome DevTools' vision emulation to check.
HSL's structure makes it easy to generate consistent palettes — fix the hue and saturation, then vary the lightness:

This pattern is how Tailwind CSS and many other design systems generate their color scales. The same hue at different lightness values creates a harmonious set that works for backgrounds, borders, and text.
While not strictly background properties, shadows contribute to the visual layer system and interact with backgrounds:
